example: 「会う」(au, to meet) takes に + person: 友達に会う (meet a friend). Note: not を — 'meet' takes the dative-like に for the encountered person
「似る」(niru, to resemble) takes に: 父に似ている (resembles father). Always used in -ている form for the state
「似合う」(niau, to suit) takes に: 君に似合う (it suits you). Subject is the clothing/style; に marks the wearer
「勝つ」(katsu, to win) takes に + opponent: 試合に勝つ (win a match), 敵に勝つ (defeat the enemy). The opponent/competition takes に
「負ける」(makeru, to lose) takes に: 試合に負けた (lost the match). Mirror of 勝つ
「慣れる」(nareru, to get used to) takes に: 新しい仕事に慣れる (get used to the new job)
「反対する」(hantai-suru, to oppose) takes に + opposed-thing: 計画に反対する (oppose the plan)
「賛成する」(sansei-suru, to agree with) takes に: 意見に賛成する (agree with the opinion). Mirror of 反対する
「出会う」(deau, to encounter) takes に: 偶然彼に出会った (I encountered him by chance). Less planned than 会う
「気づく」(kizuku, to notice) takes に: 間違いに気づいた (I noticed the mistake). Compound of 気 + つく
「触る」(sawaru, to touch) takes に: 触らないで (don't touch). Surface-level contact takes に. Compare 触れる + に (lighter touch)
「乗る」(noru, to ride/board) takes に: 電車に乗る (board the train). Vehicle/conveyance takes に
「入る」(hairu, to enter) takes に: 部屋に入る (enter the room). Distinguish from へ (directional) — に for static endpoint
「住む」(sumu, to live/reside) takes に: 東京に住んでいる (I live in Tokyo). Permanent residence takes に, not で
「泊まる」(tomaru, to stay overnight) takes に: ホテルに泊まる (stay at a hotel). Lodging location takes に
「勤める」(tsutomeru, to work for) takes に + company: 会社に勤めている (works for a company). Note: long-term employment, not 'be working' (which uses で)
「通う」(kayou, to commute) takes に: 学校に通う (commute to school)
「答える」(kotaeru, to answer) takes に + question: 質問に答える (answer the question). Distinguish from 答える + person (rare)
「頼む」(tanomu, to request) takes に + person + を + thing: 友達に頼む (ask a friend). Two-object: に for person, を for request
「送る」(okuru, to send) takes に + recipient + を + thing: 母に手紙を送る (send a letter to mother). Two-object pattern
「あげる」(ageru, to give) takes に + recipient + を + thing: 友達に本をあげる (give a book to a friend). Speaker/closer-to-speaker giving
「もらう」(morau, to receive) takes に or から + giver + を + thing: 友達に本をもらった (I got a book from a friend)
「見る」(miru, to see/watch) takes を: 映画を見る (watch a movie). Standard transitive object marker
「聞く」(kiku, to hear/listen/ask) takes を: 音楽を聞く (listen to music). When meaning 'ask' takes に + person
「待つ」(matsu, to wait for) takes を: バスを待つ (wait for the bus). NOTE: を not に — the awaited object is direct, not dative
「守る」(mamoru, to protect/keep) takes を: 約束を守る (keep a promise), 法律を守る (obey the law)
「求める」(motomeru, to seek/demand) takes を: 助けを求める (seek help)
「始める」(hajimeru, transitive: to begin) takes を: 仕事を始める (start the job). Mirror: 始まる (intransitive) takes が
「戦う」(tatakau, to fight) takes と + opponent: 敵と戦う (fight the enemy). と marks reciprocal/co-participant
「結婚する」(kekkon-suru, to marry) takes と: 彼女と結婚する (marry her). NOT を — Japanese marriage takes と for partner
「話す」(hanasu, to talk with) takes と + interlocutor: 友達と話す (talk with a friend). Distinguish from 話す + を (tell a story)
「会う」alternative form takes と: 友達と会う (meet with a friend). と is more reciprocal/social; に is more directional
「比べる」(kuraberu, to compare) takes と + compared item: 兄と比べる (compare with elder brother)
「別れる」(wakareru, to part with) takes と + person: 彼と別れた (I broke up with him)
「相談する」(soudan-suru, to consult with) takes と: 先生と相談する (consult with the teacher)
「出る」(deru, to exit) takes から + place: 家から出る (exit the house). Mirror of 入る + に
「もらう」(morau) alternative pattern takes から + source: 先生から本をもらった (I got a book from the teacher). から emphasizes origin more than に
「学ぶ」(manabu, to learn from) takes から: 先生から学ぶ (learn from the teacher). Source-of-learning takes から
「始まる」(hajimaru, intransitive: to begin) takes から for starting time: 9時から始まる (starts at 9). Static event onset
「成る」(naru, to become) takes に + new state: 大人になる (become an adult), 医者になる (become a doctor). Distinct from と+naru in formal contexts
「と+なる」(to-naru, to formally/publicly become) takes と: 結果となる (it becomes the result), 力となる (becomes a force). Formal register
「知り合う」(shiriau, to become acquainted with) takes と: 新しい人と知り合う (meet someone new). Reciprocal -au compound
「間違える」(machigaeru, to mistake/get wrong) takes を: 答えを間違える (get the answer wrong)
